fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 64 uses strictly-greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so when `len(active) == limit` the sensor is incorrectly allowed instead of denied.

constraints.intent: When active count equals limit the branch condition should be True (deny), but the strict > misses the equality case causing the sensor to be incorrectly allowed.
Checks that if active count equals limit the strict-greater-than condition still triggers denial — this is violated because > excludes equality.
In the else branch (sensor allowed), active count must be strictly less than limit; if it equals limit we should not reach here.

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result[0] is True and len(active__1) >= limit)

constraints.intent: The sensor must never be allowed when active actions reach or exceed the limit.
